danger_signals.visual_form: incoming enemy craft sprites moving downward on screen
danger_signals.meaning: active threat; player must shoot or evade

danger_signals.visual_form: stationary and mobile enemy units on terrain below flight path
danger_signals.meaning: environmental hazard; requires targeting and avoidance

danger_signals.visual_form: distinct enemy sprite designs and formations
danger_signals.meaning: organized hostile force; waves indicate escalating threat level

danger_signals.visual_form: visual loss of ship pieces/modules when hit
danger_signals.meaning: health degradation; loss of weapon upgrades increases vulnerability

safety_signals.visual_form: small capsule appearing on ground after destruction; visual attachment to Wing Galibur
safety_signals.meaning: upgraded firepower and defensive shields; collected pieces provide extra hit protection

safety_signals.visual_form: Wing Galibur transforms into flaming phoenix sprite
safety_signals.meaning: temporary invincibility period; brief safety window from all enemy fire

safety_signals.visual_form: ship pieces split into triangular formation around main craft
safety_signals.meaning: pieces become invulnerable shields; player gains tactical positioning advantage
successful survival and advancement toward mission goal

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: distinct enemy aircraft sprite designs; ground-based combat units with varied appearance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: organized wave formations; hierarchical attack patterns indicating military structure
singular player-controlled ship with modular upgrade system; phoenix transformation represents ultimate defensive capability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: sparse enemy formations; basic enemy types on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enemies move in predictable patterns; slower attack frequency

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: increased enemy density; more complex sprite formations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: coordinated wave attacks; more aggressive enemy movement and firing patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: dense enemy coverage across screen; ground and air threats simultaneously
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: concentrated fire patterns; sustained attack waves; minimal safe movement space

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: visually expanded ship with attached modules; wider visual footprint on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enhanced weapon fire spread; multi-directional attack capability; increased hit tolerance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: phoenix transformation visual; flaming aura around craft
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: all enemy fire passes through player; temporary offensive/defensive invulnerability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: modular pieces visually separated in triangular formation around main ship
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: pieces orbit/protect main craft; pieces absorb damage while main ship remains vulnerable

environmental_storytelling.example: increasing complexity and variety of Mandler Army units across stages; signals deepening conflict and escalating stakes

environmental_storytelling.technique: Ground and air threat simultaneous presence
environmental_storytelling.example: combination of airborne and terrestrial enemy units creates environmental narrative of total war scenario
modular ship upgrades represent technological accumulation and preparation against existential threat
Wing Galibur's ability to become flaming phoenix symbolizes ultimate defensive capability; invulnerability state represents peak tactical advantage in conflict
vertical scrolling through battlefield terrain communicates ongoing military campaign through territory

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: responds to player input; fires weapons at enemies; collects power-up capsules
displays attached ship pieces; wider weapon spread; increased durability
pieces orbit in triangular pattern around main ship; pieces invulnerable; main ship vulnerable
invulnerable to all damage; active offensive capability; temporary state with duration limit
form coordinated wave patterns; attack in hierarchical formations; increase density/aggression as stage progresses
stationary or slow-moving; present as environmental hazards requiring active targeting
appear on ground after enemy destruction; visual marker indicates active acquisition opportunity

notes: Terra Cresta communicates world-state primarily through enemy density, formation complexity, and threat manifestation rather than explicit HUD elements. The faction conflict is implicit: Mandler Army aggression versus humanity's (player's) technological adaptation. Safety and danger states map directly to power-up collection and transformation mechanics. Visual communication relies on sprite variation, formation patterns, and modular ship appearance changes. No explicit political/diplomatic communication system present; conflict is purely military in nature. NES version features recolored UI (gold to lilac title screen) and enhanced soundtrack compared to Japanese Famicom version, but core visual communication systems remain consistent.
